programme.title: In the Footsteps of Killers
programme.desc: Notorious cold cases are reopened. In 1993, a Lambeth Council employee was beaten to death in his Clapham home. Was it personal or were dark forces at his workplace involved?

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Jeremy and Patricia are rushed to Queen's Medical Centre, Nottingham with multiple injuries after a high-speed car crash. And Andrew has fallen three meters from a ladder at work.

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Country Lives
programme.desc: Dave and Lynn continue their farmhouse renovations inside and out. Susie and Bill start planning what to do with their garden.

programme.title: Escape to Florida
programme.desc: Things hot up in Tequesta, as fire captain David puts on a special drill for the new chief. And Holly's working her socks off to get her medieval footwear business up and running.

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Almería, Sharon Garner's feeling the pressure as she and Steve help Shropshire couple Michael and Susan find a holiday home in Spain. In Murcia, Darren and Natalie Brown search with pilot Jonathan and A&E doctor Claire to find a home for them.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sonia and Andrew are ready to make the permanent move to inland Almeria to enjoy semi-retirement in the sun. Can Laura Hamilton find five properties within their £145,000 budget?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Craig Rowe helps recently retired Helen find her dream holiday home in La Zenia, Spain. For her budget of £105,000 Helen wants a three-bedroom house or apartment with a communal pool.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman helps Keith and Heidi find their dream holiday home in southern Alicante in Spain, with enough space for their four children, six grandchildren and two dogs to visit. She lines up five great properties for their budget of £170,000.

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: Erin and JB tackle a major electrical fault; will it be 'lights out' on their driveway? Tim gets to work on a new terrace. Jon and Carmen are floored by their château's kitchen.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Newly-weds Des and Julie look for their dream home in the Spanish town of Oliva in Valencia to enjoy with their big extended family. Can Ben Hillman find them the perfect place for £105k?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: This week's competition is in and around Brighton, where first host, 47-year-old radio presenter Guy, hopes his musical and Mexican-themed menu and an air guitar competition will be a hit.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the second day in and around Brighton, and bubbly 26-year-old Emma is hosting a beach themed dinner party, but there's a last minute disaster when dessert lands in her lap.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's day three in and around Brighton, and the turn of Dutch pilot Tim to try and host his way to the prize. Tim's taking advantage of his worldly travels with a truly international menu.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's day four in and around Brighton, and personal trainer Hannah hopes to reveal a lot more about herself. And there's no shortage of surprises as layer after saucy layer is peeled away.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the final night in and around Brighton, and time for 50-year-old 'love doctor' Monti to try and cast his love spells over his guests in an attempt to win the £1000 cash prize.

programme.title: Yorkshire By The Sea
programme.desc: Whitby distillers Jess and Luke create a tasty tipple from foraged seaside ingredients. And in Scarborough, pioneering sea-farmer Wave checks out his seaweed harvest of sugar kelp.

programme.title: Steam Train Diaries
programme.desc: The iconic 'Tornado' takes us from Levisham to Pickering, wildfires threaten the railway's summer season, and guide dog puppies are welcomed aboard.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

programme.title: Murder of the Essex Boys: Blood and Betrayal
programme.desc: On 6 December 1995, Tony Tucker, Pat Tate and Craig Rolfe were murdered on a quiet country lane in Essex. Exclusive interviews with key players in the Essex crime scene and local detectives offer a detailed account of what actually happened.

programme.title: Traffic Cops

programme.title: Traffic Cops

programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.desc: During a mountain vacation, hang-gliders Craig and Mitch are trapped in a ravine; Cort learns a friend faked his own death.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Cort and Craig are trapped in an underwater cave following a major earthquake; Gina's life hangs in the balance.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Offshore oil drilling and the rescue of fishermen who claim there is a dangerous creature in the bay occupy the lifeguards.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Offshore oil drilling and the rescue of fishermen who claim there is a dangerous creature in the bay occupy the lifeguards.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Megan narrowly escapes an attacker on the beach, who then stalks her; lifeguard Shauni suffers from burnout.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: A movie producer offers Mitch a part in an action film; Shauni raises money for an animal-rescue group.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Mitch's rebellious nephew (Chance M. Corbitt) risks his life surfing in dangerous waters near a condemned pier. With David Hasselhoff.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: A gang member (Richard Coca) defies his father (Danny Trejo) and joins a program where he learns various aquatic skills. With Billy Warlock, John Allen Nelson.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Hobie helps his new friend, a homeless girl, retrieve an important item stolen from her by a vagrant.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: When Shauni decides that she and Eddie should take a break from their romance, Eddie makes a date with a woman he has never seen before.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: A teenage girl tries to impress her peers by claiming Eddie seduced her; a former lifeguard prone to daredevil adventures gets Hobie into trouble.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Mitch suspends Eddie because of the rape charges against him; Eric Turner has an accident while hang gliding.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Mitch (David Hasselhoff) is seduced by a woman (Shannon Tweed) he just rescued, but he doesn't realize she is a murderer.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Mitch (David Hasselhoff) attends his high-school class reunion with his ex-wife, Gayle (Wendie Malick); Eddie (Billy Warlock) finds an abandoned baby. Singer Billy Vera also guest stars.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.sub-title: Now Sit Right Back and You'll Hear a Tale

programme.title: Lon le Linda

programme.title: Cleasan Ataidh & Tilidh
programme.desc: Abney is making porridge bread, but he just cannot get the recipe right. He ends up with lots of spare loaves and nothing to do with them.

programme.title: Daolag-bhreac is Seillean

programme.title: Strì

programme.title: An Saoghal Droil aig Pòl Ploc
programme.desc: The water in the Spuds' flat is off, and having not had a bath in weeks, Dave is sent down to the basement to find the problem.

programme.title: An Saoghal Droil aig Pòl Ploc
programme.desc: Dave's pet goldfish returns from the dead to wreak havoc.

programme.title: Na Speuran
programme.desc: Astronaut Emily shares some interesting facts about the sun. Tha rudan inntinneach aig an Speuradair Emily ri innse dhuinn mun ghrèin.

programme.title: Hilda
programme.desc: Tha Hilda is Flòraidh airson dearbhadh nach e an Dràgon aig Eilean a' Choire a tha a' draghadh sìos bhàtaichean anns a' phort. Hilda and Flòraidh want to prove that it is not the Dragon of Cauldron Island who is pulling ships down in the harbour.

programme.title: Gàrraidhean Mòra
programme.desc: Tha Màiri Rodgers a' gabhail cuairt tro Ghàrradh Threave faisg air Caisteal Dhùghlais. Mairi Rodgers explores Threave Garden near Castle Douglas. She gets some tips on caring for glasshouse plants and finds about the honey being made at the garden.

programme.title: SpeakGaelic
programme.desc: Learner Mirren Buchanan from Thurso chats to Joy in the studio, and Calum Maclean visits Ayrshire where he gets to know some powerful local inhabitants.

programme.title: An La

programme.title: Suas gu Deas
programme.desc: Tha'n fhèis Barralive a' tilleadh, agus tha turas-mara inntinneach aig Ruairidh agus Peter. The Barralive music festival is back and Ruairidh and Peter have an eventful trip to sea.

programme.title: Cocain is na Klondykers
programme.desc: Sreath aithriseach a' sgrùdadh glacadh dhrogaichean cho mòr 's a bha a-riamh ann an Alba. Documentary investigating one of the biggest drug seizures in Scottish history.

programme.title: Cùram
programme.desc: Tha sinn a' coinneachadh ri Dòmhnall Iain MacIlleathain à Solas ann an Uibhist a Tuath, aig a bheil cerebral palsy. We meet Donald John Maclean from Sollas in North Uist who has cerebral palsy.

programme.title: Rurachd (Foraging)

programme.desc: Drama Gaidhlig steidhichte ann an Leodhas. Gaelic soap based on the Isle of Lewis.

programme.title: Fraochy Bay
programme.desc: Local lobster fishermen are cast away by a tall tale and a tuneful encounter.

programme.title: Na Clangers
programme.desc: Tha rudeigin a' tighinn - abair iongnadh! Something is coming, what a surprise!

programme.desc: Hikari wants to grow rice and is looking for help from a rice farmer, Mr Yamanaka.

programme.title: Belle and Sebastian

programme.title: Seonaidh
programme.desc: The flock hear the enticing tunes of an ice cream van approaching and quickly hatch a plan to divert it into their field. While the driver seeks help, the flock have great fun with an endless supply of ice cream. But will they be able to cover their tracks before they're discovered?

programme.title: Uaine

programme.title: Bogaisean is Gumbalan

programme.title: Miann na Maighdinn-mara

programme.title: Mise is ADHD
programme.desc: Recent years have seen a large rise in the numbers of women being diagnosed with ADHD. Suzie Roberts is one of them. Through meeting others with ADHD, Suzie hears powerful personal testimonies about the impact that it has had on their lives, from difficulty in staying in a job, to mental health struggles, to ending up in the criminal justice system.

And for Christian Horne, the wet and windy summer means he is taking advantage of the big event of the season - Euro 2024.

programme.title: Teen Predator/Online Killer
programme.desc: 20-year-old Alexander McCartney, already known to police, is arrested and his devices seized after a 12-year-old girl in Scotland reports a serious catfishing abuse incident online. McCartney gives a 'no comment' interview while in custody and without enough physical evidence to charge him, he is released on bail. Back home at his parents' house, McCartney's bail conditions are supposed to prevent him from using devices that can connect to the internet.

programme.title: Teen Predator/Online Killer
programme.desc: With hundreds of thousands of images of child abuse discovered on McCartney's devices, the race is on to get him remanded into custody. McCartney is re-arrested in the early hours of the morning, at which time police discover McCartney's phone on his bedside table, where his final victim was abused on Snapchat just hours before. The U.S. Department of Homeland Security notify the police in Northern Ireland of two more catfishing victims, this time the victims are in the United States.

programme.title: Teen Predator/Online Killer
programme.desc: As police piece together the last few minutes of Cimarron's life, they realise McCartney was still online threatening her when she tragically shot herself. Police and prosecutors move quickly to establish if there's a case for manslaughter. Police from Northern Ireland travel to West Virginia to conduct the manslaughter investigation where they learn that Cimarron's own family had no idea why she took her own life. In a tragic turn, they learn that 18 months after her death.

programme.desc: The freedivers take on the scubas in a race to find a precious lost tankard for a heartbroken son. programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Thirty players compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day.

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Four more contenders take to the black chair. Clive Myrie asks questions on the films of Judy Garland, The Cure, Hi-de-Hi! and the French Wars of Religion.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio.

programme.title: Under the Vines
programme.desc: Daisy is ecstatic over Griffin's visit, while everyone is fed up with Louis' self-pity following his marriage breakup; Nic asks Daisy and the Oakley gang for support.

programme.title: Sicily: The Wonder of the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Historian Michael Scott continues his journey through Sicily tracing the islands story through the arrival of the Muslim Arabs and then the Normans. Times in which religious and cultural tolerance was the order of the day.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: From the beautiful surrounds of Warwick Castle the finds are valuable cigarette cards, ancient Peruvian figures and working drawings from a celebrated furniture maker.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The show comes from the Blickling estate in north Norfolk. Five antiques experts are on hand to find a selection of objects to take to auction. David Harper is excited by a Rolex oyster watch, and Paul Martin learns more about Lord Lothian.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Cheryl Baker, Chris Bavin, Axel Blake and Sophie Willan take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman.

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's Belgian rail journey continues in the Dutch-speaking region of Flanders. He discovers Antwerp's historic diamond industry and visits a Van Eyck masterpiece in Ghent.

programme.desc: Marcus wants to impress the locals with a French version of the classic Sunday roast and find out how they enjoy their take on the sandwich.

programme.desc: Darren Harriott introduces Jessica Fostekew and Stephen Bailey to the famous stage.

programme.desc: The team stumble on a souvenir of the Miami Dolphins' inaugural 1966 year and hear from a woman who claims she has unseen footage of President Frank D Roosevelt.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: The Pawn Stars set their sights on an 1870s Volcanic pistol, stumble upon a rare World War II gas mask and negotiate a deal on a giant Steiff bear.

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Upcycler extraordinaire EJ Osborne is at Woking Recycling Centre, on a mission to save three tip-bound items that he can restore, rejuvenate and sell on for a profit. To help him turn trash into cash, EJ enlists the help of upcycler Sarah Peterson to give an outdated telephone cabinet a radical revamp, while woodworkers Josh and Oli are tasked with turning a tired tool box into something special.

programme.title: Junk and Disorderly
programme.desc: Henry Cole and top restorers Allen Millyard and Guy Willison scour the country, challenging themselves to make money out of buying and selling other people's automotive junk and classic collectibles.

programme.title: Storage Wars Texas
programme.desc: The bidders journey to the Dallas suburb of Waxahachie, Ricky and Bubba score some green, Mary's scandalous find could earn her a huge profit and Marry gets a buzz from a hand-powered machine.

programme.title: Storage Wars Texas
programme.desc: The bidders return to the city of DeSoto, Jenny seeks out raw materials, Kenny and Matt fight over industrial tools and Mary's find leads her to the state's oldest Italian restaurant.

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Modern imaging technology and analysis from military and intelligence professionals, as well as leading scientists, investigate mysteries from strange geological occurrences to odd man-made structures as seen by more than 4,000 orbiting satellites.

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Six breathtaking images, six unfathomable mysteries; from a possible smoke screen over North Korea, to two forgotten pyramids in Egypt.

programme.title: Queens of Ancient Egypt
programme.desc: The reign of Cleopatra II was even more turbulent than that of her famous, later namesake, Cleopatra VII. This great queen saved her dynasty from being destroyed by hatred and strife.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: The guys stumble upon a Cy Young baseball card from 1909, some rare handcrafted Pinocchio dolls and a harmonica that once belonged to Aerosmith's Steven Tyler.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: The Pawn Stars set their sights on a musket made by cotton gin inventor Eli Whitney. They also come across a golf ball owned by President Lyndon B Johnson.

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is at the Recycling Centre in Witley, on the lookout for three items she can salvage, spruce up and sell on for a profit. programme.desc: Live from the Roots and Fruits theatre, it's Cauliflower creaking away as she grows and sings her catchy `Cauliflower Creak' song.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Children's animation series. Coco discovers Bing's dressing-up box so they both dress up. Coco is Princess Cococapono and Bing is, of course, King Bing. But there is only one crown and both need it to complete their outfits. When the crown gets broken, Flop helps them to make new crowns out of the broken pieces.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: It is a windy day in Teletubbyland and Dipsy's hat blows away, but Po helps Dipsy to get his hat back; in Tummy Tales, children have fun playing on a windy beach.

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Mo is always there to help, but Tee is getting bigger and wants to do everything by himself.

programme.title: Supertato

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing has broken his arm and is struggling to do his usual activities. Petting Arlo hurts, playing with blocks hurts, and even drinking juice hurts. But when Bing and Sula draw together it doesn't hurt.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Bo is tired and falls asleep in the middle of hunting for Fabio's lost inflatable fruit. It's up to Mojo to finish the job - without waking up poor, exhausted Bo!

programme.title: Hey Duggee's Squirrel Club
programme.desc: Today at Squirrel Club, Duggee forgets how to woof, Duggly comes to visit, Roly celebrates Potato Time, and Billy teaches everyone the Bugatron dance!

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels notice that Happy looks and seems different - he is frowning, his smile is upside down, and he's wearing new trainers! When they check in on him, Happy is frustrated, but he can't seem to identify where his frustration is coming from. They turn to Duggee for help, who uses his feeling cards to explain an array of feelings to Happy. All the Squirrels help by relaying their past experiences and the feelings that they evoked.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey and Bingo teach Unicorse good manners, but things go awry when Unicorse eats a tick.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: The Heelers take a late afternoon walk by the river, but Bingo decides she's too tired to walk any further and needs a piggyback. To distract her, Mum and Dad invent fun games and get her little legs moving again.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Glitch wants to ski, so the Grimbots give Table Mountain a snowy peak, which threatens its rare plants.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of the Mystery Plum Thief
programme.desc: When Peter tries to get the last plum from Mr McGregor's tree, he finds that a mysterious thief has beaten him to it.

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: Peso and Kwazii venture down to the midnight zone to help a hurt creature. What they find however is a shipwreck, haunted by a spooky-looking long-armed squid.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Squarey, we're not in Numberland any more! Four visits Flatland, where the flat shapes live, and becomes a real square. Learn about 2D shapes with the Numberblocks.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: It's O's birthday, but R has forgotten to get him a present! Can she magic up a big wheel, a helter skelter and a hall of mirrors from thin air?

programme.title: Nick Cope's Popcast
programme.desc: The neighbours have left a 'please take' box outside their house, so Nick picks out some exciting things. The little crochet doll he finds looks a bit sad. Can Norman and the Popcasters help write a song to cheer it up?

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.sub-title: Top of the Pups: Five Speckled Frogs
programme.desc: We sing a calming song about counting frogs with Mrs Weaver.

programme.title: Playtime Towers
programme.desc: Bella and Lift visit the Fortress of Fun, where Kingsley and Queenie have fun looking for peas with the Playtimers in their Pea Party game.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing and Sula are in the park playing at being giants and splashing in puddles with their big boots. As they play, the puddles get bigger and bigger until Bing slips over in the mud. Amma comes to the rescue with a giant warm bath and a giant carroty bagel.

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say `teddy' and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We see lots of different teddies, a big teddy, some small teddies, a rainbow teddy and some cuddly teddies. There's a fluffy teddy, a flower teddy and even a flying teddy. We also get to meet Dee's special teddy.

programme.title: Lu & the Bally Bunch
programme.desc: Lu and Declan race their home toys and Declan accidentally bumps Lu's toy, Emma, into the shelf. He and Lu get a bandaid for Emma and create a toy hospital.

programme.title: Kiri and Lou
programme.desc: Kiri, Lou and Sorry are playing tickle monster. They are very surprised when the grumpy Ichtheo wants to join in, but he turns out to be the best monster of all.

programme.title: Froglets
programme.desc: On Alex's birthday, the Froglets lift off in a hot-air balloon for a high-flying party.

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos learn all about down when Toodloo gets on a runaway roller-skate. Back in the Boat, the Twirlywoo Screen creates a course for marbles to roll down.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Maddie meets two friends to play games at the park and Mr Tumble dances with the toys.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: It's time to wake up, but Tinky Winky isn't feeling well. Soon after, Tinky Winky feels better, but now Dipsy, Laa-Laa and Po don't feel well.

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome babies Tobias, Inam, Isaac, Enya, Hawa, Jean and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their babies at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Inside everyone's bags in this episode are shakers and rattles which are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding baby appropriate shaker or rattle inside a fabric bag or pillowcase.

programme.title: Tiny Tunes
programme.desc: Lisa and the children enjoy a teddy bears' picnic and sign and sing When I Was One.

programme.title: I Can Grow It
programme.desc: It is a busy day down in the garden, as Aaron, Adrian and Nelly are looking for some minibeasts! Their journey starts when they lift some logs and stones to see what is underneath, uncovering a family of woodlice scuttling around.

programme.title: Monster Makes
programme.desc: Murphy is in the middle of a mega clean-up in the attic, but he can't find Rebecca's lost hat. His magic cleaning machine goes wrong and causes mayhem, but it does give him a monster make idea - a paper banana skin! The Mini Monsters create wonderful scrap bag monsters, while Rebecca's grandpa tries to create a new ice cream flavour and a dreamy creamy sundae. The Recycling Express brings an old box with a window, which Murphy and Rebecca transform into a terrific box truck.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Ginger the Tricarrotops explores the world of the crunchiest era, going on adventures throughout the colourful Vegesaur Valley.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Dad brings Bluey and Bingo on a wagon ride to the park, and Bluey is keen to try the monkey bars. But her patience is tested when Dad continues to stop and chat with other grown-ups.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: After hearing a strange sound, the Squirrels turn to Duggee to help identify its source. They learn about sound waves, and that you can listen for different qualities in the sounds, such as if it's loud, quiet, high or low. Describing the sound can help you work out where it's coming from, so Duggee lets the Squirrels close their eyes as they listen to sounds and make their guesses.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Howdy diddly diddly do. This song is all about all the different things that can be learnt with Duggee to earn badges. From singing to painting, dancing, playing and making friends too.

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk to the beach to make funny faces in the sand. Along the way, they count the boats in the harbour, see a heron take off, spot a boat that looks like a whale and find a tiny crab in the rockpool.

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Henry and his little sister Sophie make crowns and become King Henry and Queen Sophie. Along with their noble knights, Mummy and Mama, they head to the local park on a royal quest to rescue their toy turtle Pebbles, who is trapped in a castle.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Nyama is a type of stew from Zimbabwe. It uses a special type of young cabbage leaf called spring greens, which Pippa adds to her stew along with lots of other delicious ingredients. Pippa works hard preparing all the spices, chicken and shallots.

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie is finding out about repeating patterns. She visits a butterfly sanctuary to learn how the life cycle of a butterfly works. Maddie uses her special microscope camera to show us the marvellous moment when a tiny caterpillar hatches out of its egg, and we see three Owl butterflies emerge from their pupa and spread their wings for the first time. Then Maddie heads to a wallpaper factory to learn how a repeat pattern is designed and the different colours are printed.

programme.title: Dog Squad
programme.desc: Disability assistance dog Tinks visits Clip-Clop Stables with Charli, where they meet a very tall horse called Mist, who is taking Charli for her first ever horse riding lesson.

programme.title: Go Wild
programme.sub-title: Aneeshwar and Lizzie: Vulture and Brittlestar
programme.desc: Aneeshwar and Lizzie meet a vulture called Ethel. Vultures have amazing eyesight and can spot food from high up in the sky. To meet their next animal, Aneeshwar and Lizzie must go far down below. The brittlestar lives underwater in the ocean.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle is banned from going anywhere near Sommersby School, so Gramps is at the Brooklyn-Bells' house to cheer him up. They play a game together on the front drive. Gramps and Waffle run in circles quickly, then slowly, before falling to the ground with laughter. Mrs Hobbs appears at her front door. She might have known it would be Gramps and Waffle making the racket. Mrs Hobbs reveals she's doggy day-caring Connie and Ruby's dog, Jelly. Gramps and Waffle can't believe it.

programme.title: Puffin Rock
programme.desc: When Oona and Baba find a sweet little lamb, Oona decides that the he must be lost. After all, lambs usually follow their mums everywhere, don't they?

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: The Small Potatoes hop on board the Potato Train and chug through town, greeting local town's potatoes along the way.

programme.title: Yukee
programme.desc: When Toad finds himself with a case of puddle hiccups, it's up to Yukee and the rest of the garden gang to find a cure.

programme.title: Maddie and Triggs
programme.desc: Mum and Dad are heading out, which means Maddie and Triggs get to spend the evening with Uncle George. Let us just hope they save enough energy for tidying up.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels are having fun playing games but are surprised to find out that Betty didn't always enjoy playing. Since her first day at Squirrel Club, she has always impressed everyone with her confidence and independence, but because she had grown up playing alone, she didn't know how to join in with the others. Luckily, Duggee had a great idea to bring all the Squirrels together. As a group, they performed the Squirrel Club song about their friends and what they're good at.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Five and friends discover a hole that makes their heads fall off. Learn how to count up and down and how to add to five with the Numberblocks.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: From A to Z, every Alphablock has fun discovering the magic of wordplay. When the Alphablocks can't decide between rock and pop, it's time for a battle of the bands to see which is best.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.sub-title: We're All Friends: Get Set Gardening
programme.desc: Get ready for some gardening with Justin, Mr Tumble and their friends.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies bounce on the Tubby Bouncy. Po bounces as high as a cloud, then Laa-Laa bounces higher, Dipsy bounces even higher and Tinky Winky bounces highest of all.

programme.title: Ranger Hamza's Eco Quest
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers discover how shells play an important role in the environment. They find out that shells help protect the creatures that live inside them and that many animals grow their own shells, which get bigger as they grow.

programme.title: Football Fantastics
programme.desc: Can Evie, Taran and Jaz help Mr Parks rebuild his bug hotel in time for the competition?

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: Grace shows off three amazing machines that are all like animals. Grace goes for a ride in a vehicle like a shark, shows off a machine that flies by flapping its wings like a bird, and then goes for a drive in a big robot insect.

programme.title: Dodge's Pup School
programme.desc: It's the first day of Pup School. Dodge overcomes his first day nerves as he meets lots of new friends. The pups settle into their class activities with their new teacher Miss Collie.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Ginger joins the baby Pea-Rexes on their expedition to find dinner and ends up eating something too hot to handle, which alerts the Bananaraptors.

programme.title: Octonauts: Above & Beyond
programme.desc: Paani saves a newborn goose, then seeks the Octonauts' help to decipher its behaviour.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: Peter Rabbit wants to grow up to be just like his late father - he has big paws to fill. But with his father's journal and the help of his best friends Benjamin and Lily, Peter is ready for some fun and delightful new discoveries.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels are playing hide-and-seek and come across a pink snorting fluffy animal, a scruffelump. The Squirrels are excited and want to keep the scruffemlump as their pet, but Duggee is wary. Keeping a pet is a lot of work. They need feeding, cleaning, exercising and much more that will keep you very busy. But they are wonderful too and can bring a lot of love to life.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: Vida and friends move a piano all the way across Sweetwood to save Pippen's concert!

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Follow the adventures of Supertato, the world's first potato superhero.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Ted the bus wants to be faster, and Mojo swops every top trying to make it happen. But it's Bo's homemade rocket that gives Ted the boost he needs- all the way into space!

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey and Bingo teach Unicorse good manners, but things go awry when Unicorse eats a tick.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: The Heelers take a late afternoon walk by the river, but Bingo decides she's too tired to walk any further and needs a piggyback. To distract her, Mum and Dad invent fun games and get her little legs moving again.

programme.title: Zog
programme.desc: The fearsome Great-Gnanna Gnome has a case of bottom-fungal-itis. Zog and the doctors must figure out how to treat the mushroom without incurring Gnanna's anger.

programme.title: Balamory
programme.desc: Penny brings a dinosaur fossil to the nursery, while Bonnie and Hamish plan a secret dino dance. But mysterious footprints have Penny wondering if there's a real dino on the island.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: The Brooklyn-Bells are on holiday with Gramps, so Waffle is staying at Angela's dog kennels for a couple of nights. During a face-to-face call with Waffle, the Brooklyn-Bells check their wonder dog is settling in and ensure he has his favourite snuggly toy with him, PegStar. Angela tries to settle Waffle but he whines, he's missing his Brooklyn-Bell family and looks at their photo on the wall of his kennel.

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard
programme.desc: Rafi's magic messes with Finn's show, but teamwork helps him prove himself.

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.sub-title: It's Time to Build a Bug Hotel
programme.desc: JoJo, Gran Gran and Cynthia build a bug hotel in Cynthia's garden and welcome some new residents. JoJo learns that different insects are attracted to different materials and environments in the bug hotel. Ladybirds like straw, beetles like dry leaves and bees like nooks and crannies. Gran Gran gives JoJo her own mini bug hotel for the insects in her own garden.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Inspired by a nature documentary, Sarah and Duck attempt to take photographs of birds.

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: A meteor shower is about to hit the Clangers' planet. Major and Small shelter on the Iron Chicken's nest, while the others find cover in the living cave. But when the shower stops, they find that a giant meteor has fallen and blocked the double doors to the inside. With Major and Small not yet back, Mother, Granny and Tiny have to think of a way to remove the boulder themselves. They make their way to Major's workshop to see if they can invent a machine to help.

programme.title: Beddybyes
programme.desc: MeMo and BaBa have fun playing tickle chase. BaBa wants to stop playing, and with the help of the Yummies, MeMo learns that there is a time and a place for tickles.

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Pontipines and the Wottingers are out for a walk in the garden. Mrs Pontipine spots the Ninky Nonk through her binoculars. Makka Pakka, Upsy Daisy and Igglepiggle all join the Pontipines.

programme.title: Cbeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: A different story read by a special guest each night just before bedtime.

programme.desc: Sam is overjoyed when Rebecca invites him back to her place. Have all of his Christmases come at once?

Ambitious business school graduate Lisa bought the restaurant when she was 23 but five years later, she's starting to think she picked the wrong venture. Lisa's clueless in the kitchen, doesn't eat the food she serves and has no control over what happens anywhere in the restaurant.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Swansea and Bridgend, an Italian menu is overshadowed by terrible table manners and tactless banter, plus there's a starter of chicken lettuce boats - and a final showdown at the table.

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Greg and Georgie plan to convert a cavernous, dilapidated, 35-year-old barn in Georgie's parents' garden in Kent into a peaceful safe haven. But budget-squeezing building work soon sets in.

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.sub-title: Mobile Cinema, Caravan and Shipping Container
programme.desc: George catches up with designer Max McMurdo, who converted a shipping container into a garden office. This time Max has gone back to his old middle school in Bedford to create a classroom out of two rusting shipping containers.

programme.desc: A PT Master is forced to do what he has never done: done.

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show
programme.desc: US actor and singer Josh Groban hands over his phone to play Send to All and Michael heads to Newport for The Midnight Gameshow. Also featuring music from chart-topper Paloma Faith, and legendary funk and soul singer Nile Rogers and Chic perform.

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show
programme.desc: `Strictly' star Anton Du Beke hands over his phone for Send to All, `Love Island' stars Dani Dyer and Samira Mighty help prank the latest Unexpected Star as the Theatre Royal is turned into a cinema, and Gino D'Acampo gets a rude awakening when he becomes the first celebrity to play The Midnight Gameshow. Also featuring music from pop superstar Cheryl.

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica and Chandler disagree over how to redecorate Rachel's old room, and despite Phoebe's admonitions, Ross makes a drastic decision about Rachel.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Joey Loses His Insurance
programme.desc: After learning that his health insurance has lapsed because he has not worked enough as an actor, Joey suffers a painful hernia, and Rachel finally finds out that Ross never annulled their marriage.

programme.title: Mr Bean

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: Mr Bean goes to the launderette where he loses his trousers once again. He then plays a round of mini golf but has trouble keeping his ball on the green.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Debra convinces Ray to be an optimist, he can't handle being the only upbeat person in the family.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Robert and Ray fight over a stray bulldog similar to the one Ray was forced to give up as a child.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Ray's parents bother everyone with their meddling, the neighbours turn to him to make them stop.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: After she is interviewed for a book about the family, Debra decides she's boring compared to her husband's family.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra and Ray wonder why they ever moved across the street from his overbearing parents.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Chicken Pox
programme.desc: Phoebe's submariner boyfriend visits New York on leave from the Navy. Unfortunately, Phoebe catches chicken pox at baby Ben's first birthday party. Meanwhile, when Monica realises that Richard has no real neuroses, she worries that she is too strange for him.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Barry and Mindy's Wedding
programme.desc: When Rachel serves as a bridesmaid at the wedding of her ex-fiancé Barry and her friend Mindy, she relives the nightmare of her own failed wedding day. Meanwhile, Chandler becomes enamoured with a woman he meets on the internet, but is surprised when he finally meets her.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Princess Leia Fantasy
programme.desc: Monica is distraught after her break-up with Richard. Phoebe unsuccessfully tries to raise her spirits, but ultimately it's Mr Geller who comforts his daughter. Elsewhere, Chandler and Janice begin seeing each other again and Joey can't stand it. The two plan a day of fun to try to overcome Joey's aversions for Chandler's sake. Meanwhile, Rachel tells Phoebe and Monica about Ross's Princess Leia fantasy.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where No One's Ready
programme.desc: In a race against the clock, Ross tries to get the gang dressed and ready for a black-tie benefit at the museum.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica decides the best way to get over Richard is to keep herself busy. She develops a `jam plan', hoping the distraction of cooking will divert her attention. After making jars and jars of jam Monica decides she still wants to have a baby, so she begins searching for a sperm donor, and comes across Joey's file. Elsewhere, Phoebe is mistakenly stalked by a man who thinks she's Ursula, and Chandler receives dating advice.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Metaphorical Tunnel
programme.desc: Ross panics when he sees his son with a Barbie doll, and Chandler's fear of commitment surfaces in his relationship with Janice.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Joey Moves Out
programme.desc: Everyone at Monica's dad's birthday party finds it amusing that their middle-aged friend, eye-doctor Richard Burke, is dating a woman in her 20s - until Monica's parents learn that it is their daughter. Meanwhile, Chandler and Joey row over a dirty spoon. Is the apartment still big enough for both of them?

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Eddie Moves In
programme.desc: With Joey in his new apartment, Chandler gets a troublesome new flatmate, Eddie. Meanwhile, Phoebe makes a video for `Smelly Cat', and Ross and Monica find it nearly impossible to live together.

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show
programme.desc: Michael McIntyre looks back over his favourite surprises and pranks from the last four series. Ed Balls, Alexander Armstrong and Carol Vorderman discuss what it was like to hand over their phone in Celebrity Send to All.

programme.desc: Set inside the offices of the Nation Building Authority, a newly created government organisation responsible for overseeing major infrastructure projects, Utopia explores that moment when bureaucracy and grand dreams collide.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Metaphorical Tunnel
programme.desc: Ross panics when he sees his son with a Barbie doll, and Chandler's fear of commitment surfaces in his relationship with Janice.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe's attempts to introduce her visiting half brother Frank Jr to the delights of city life leave him thinking that she works in a brothel. Meanwhile, Ross lists five famous women that he would like to date. To his surprise, actress Isabella Rossellini, one of the women he rejected while narrowing down his list, pops in to Central Perk.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: When Janice asks whether any of the friends have ever dated one another the gang has a flashback to three years ago (one year before the pilot was supposed to have happened) when everyone's lives were beginning to come together. Phoebe moves out of Monica's apartment, Chandler searches for a new roommate - ending up with Joey, Monica bumps into Rachel, and Ross learns that Carol is a lesbian.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Race Car Bed
programme.desc: When Monica buys a new bed from the Mattress King, the wrong one is delivered. Meanwhile, Joey sees Janice kissing her husband, and Rachel's father clashes with Ross.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Giant Poking Device
programme.desc: Chandler and Janice reach a turning point in their relationship, while Monica accidentally injures Ben and tries to hide it from Ross.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: On Thanksgiving, the friends form teams and play American football. Ross and Monica relive their sibling rivalry by going head-to-head as opposing team captains. Rachel is upset that Ross didn't choose her for his team, and Joey tries to outwit his foe Chandler. Meanwhile, the turkey is left stewing while the losing team calls for a rematch.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel wonders if she'll ever get another job after she quits Central Perk, Joey's new job selling Christmas trees upsets Phoebe, and Ross accidentally breaks a little girl's leg.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Chandler Can't Remember Which Sister
programme.desc: Ross is suspicious about Rachel's possible job offer from a good-looking stranger, while Chandler can't remember which of Joey's many attractive sisters he kissed at a party.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With All the Jealousy
programme.desc: Ross becomes increasingly jealous of Rachel's sexy new colleague, Mark, and gets the wrong end of the stick when he spies on Rachel at her office. Meanwhile, Monica is enamoured with a sexy busboy who writes love poems for her, and Joey gets himself into trouble by faking his CV in order to get a part in a Broadway musical.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Monica and Richard Are Just Friends
programme.desc: Monica bumps into Richard in a video store and they decide to work on their friendship, but the more time they spend together, the more sexual their relationship becomes. Meanwhile, Phoebe dates an athlete who unintentionally keeps exposing himself by wearing loose shorts, and Rachel agrees to read `The Shining' if Joey will read `Little Women'.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe is reunited with her former singing partner, Leslie, a failed television commercial jingle writer who wants to use Phoebe's signature song `Smelly Cat' in an advertisement for cat litter. Meanwhile, Ross becomes jealous when Rachel agrees to go to a fashion lecture with Mark, a former colleague who has just left for a new job, and Chandler meets a beautiful woman, Ginger, who has a prosthetic leg.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross and Rachel Take a Break
programme.desc: Rachel's job prevents her from celebrating her anniversary with Ross. Wanting more time to focus on her new job, she suggests the two take a break from their relationship. Convinced that Rachel has dumped him for one of her workmates, Ross hooks up with a woman from the copy shop that Joey and Chandler have been after.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross tries to prevent Rachel from learning his disturbing secret. Meanwhile, Monica, Phoebe, Chandler and Joey spend a night eavesdropping.

programme.title: The Waterboy
programme.desc: A lowly water boy for a local college football team is the butt of everyone's jokes until a coach realises that he might just be the team's next star player.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Bradley and Barney Walsh are back on the road for another epic road trip, and this time they're in for a real culture shock as they head further afield to Latin America. The boys are in Mexico, where they take on Lucha Libre wresting.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The Walshes continue their Latin American road trip. They're still in Mexico and on the side of a mountain - we rejoin them to see if Bradley can complete the final part of a challenge which requires him to swing across the top of the mountain with nothing but a terrifying drop below him.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and his team of dealers are in Carlisle today where Helen Gardiner tries to fathom out the purpose of a huge bronze table-piece, Stewart Hofgartner is keen to buy an out-of-fashion smoker's cabinet and Tim Hogarth is bewildered when he is presented with a case of wine.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson is in Liverpool's Anglican Cathedral to celebrate the 1.5 million pounds the public have taken in cash since the antiques show began, he recalls some of the best auction results, biggest bargains and the boldest sellers ever to visit the Dealers' Den.

programme.desc: Gordon travels to Philadelphia to visit a Mexican restaurant called Zocalo. Greg and Mary Russell loved the place so much that they pooled their resources and bought it. However, it seems that Cupid's arrow had a poisoned tip: the restaurant has become an albatross around their necks.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Barry and Adam bond over the discovery of a TV channel, and Beverly involves herself in her father's dating life.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride: Revisited

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Prince is smitten with Vicky, but does she feel the same? Warren makes a chilling discovery, and Nancy reaches breaking point.

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Mercedes sees a side to Jenson that she doesn't like, and Myra finds a hidden camera in the house. Elsewhere, things look up for one family, while panic sets in for another.

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube
programme.desc: A short clip from Rude Tube's naughty little sister Rude-ish Tube, featuring food and animals.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon visits Italian bistro Charlie's in La Verne, Southern California, which was bought in 2010 by Tatiana Leiva, her mother and her sister. Tatiana didn't actually put any money into the purchase herself, but her hard-working mother and sister did because they felt they were helping to fulfil Tatiana's dream. They even put up their houses as collateral! Unfortunately, soon Charlie's was in trouble.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to Baltimore to try to turn around a restaurant that is suffering because its owner, Denise Whiting, has managed to alienate the surrounding community. When Denise opened Café Hon in 1992 it was a roaring success, but as a result of her unwavering business ambition the local community decided to boycott the restaurant, leaving Denise frustrated by falling sales.

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.desc: The newly-weds are brought back to earth with a bump when they move in together and start facing the challenges - and arguments - of regular married life.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Jay and Manny set out to cut down their own Christmas tree. Meanwhile, Gloria is on edge when her mother visits. And Mitchell does last-minute shopping to find the perfect gift for Lily.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Phil ends up in jail after tricking Luke into taking a dance class. Meanwhile, Jay and Gloria plan birthday parties for Manny and Joe. Later, Mitch and Cam try to book a wedding venue.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge and Homer celebrate their 11th anniversary, but find that the sexual spark is gone. Meanwhile, Bart and Lisa go treasure hunting and find an alternative ending to a classic film.

programme.sub-title: How I Spent My Strummer Vacation
programme.desc: Marge and the kids send Homer off to rock `n' roll camp to realise his dream of being a rock star.

programme.sub-title: Bart vs Lisa vs the 3rd Grade

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Peralta and Santiago's ongoing bet about who can make the most arrests comes to an embarrassing conclusion. Meanwhile, Boyle receives a medal after he's shot in the line of duty.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: For his first task back in the field, Sergeant Jeffords prepares to join Peralta and Boyle in infiltrating a gym where steroids are being sold illegally.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: When Claire lets Phil take charge of Halloween, he decides to do something different than her usual fright-fest theme, Jay wants to be Prince Charming for Halloween -- complete with a full head of hair and Cam becomes overwhelmed.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Haley tries to land a job with an influential stylist, while Gloria hires a hot Spanish teacher for Manny.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Claire has her doubts when Phil steps up to the plate to cook Thanksgiving dinner. Plus, roles are reversed after Cam is forced to play bad cop.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Jay and Gloria celebrate their anniversary but can't decide on what kind of gift to give. The Dunphys, meanwhile, join Alex on her college tour.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart wages war against Milhouse when they are enrolled in different activity clubs.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer and Marge permit Moe to babysit Maggie after she takes a shine to the bartender.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Once Ned and Homer's bounty hunting partnership takes a turn for the worse, Homer becomes the hunted, as Ned is on the prowl for him after he skips bail. Meanwhile, Marge mistakenly takes a job at an erotic bakery.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A believer in the mantra finders keepers, a sticky-fingered Bart steals actor Denis Leary's phone and uses it for pranking purposes.

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: The police give the Osbornes some worrying news. Two residents decide to move forward together. And when Warren steps in to help Mercedes, will sparks re-ignite?

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Springfield decides to install a monorail system, Homer gets the job as conductor, but has no idea what he's doing, although he does get to wear a cape. When the monorail opens, all hell breaks loose and it's down to Homer and Bart to save the passengers.

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.desc: After an explosive meeting with an expert, one couple are left to pick up the pieces of their relationship. But is another pair's marriage already beyond fixing?

programme.title: Celebs Go Dating
programme.desc: PK Humble gets himself in trouble with the agents before going on a date. Stephen meets Claira for a make-or-break chat. Will Lucinda feel flutters with Oli?

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britain's sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the week's biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson leads people through the prickly business of choosing a partner naked. First into the studio is 21-year-old Dom, from Hampshire, who's been nominated for the show by his mum, in a desperate attempt to break his dismal dating record.

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Business development manager Emily and part-time model Matt look like a good match, until talk turns to aliens, and 73-year-old Michael's roaming eye gets him into trouble.

programme.title: Celebs Go Dating
programme.desc: PK Humble gets himself in trouble with the agents before going on a date. Stephen meets Claira for a make-or-break chat. Will Lucinda feel flutters with Oli?

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to Fremont, California, to try to save a restaurant that is tearing the owners' marriage apart. Saki Kavouniaris took over the failing Spin-a-Yarn restaurant in 1995 and began to turn it into a success. He fell in love with a waitress, but the restaurant and the marriage are now failing.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Shane Evans is COO and co-founder of Massage Heights, one of the biggest massage therapy chains in North America. She goes undercover posing as a participant in a reality TV contest who is trying to win money to fund her own business.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Bruce `Two Dogs' Bozsum of Mohegan Sun, a Native American gaming and entertainment empire owned and operated by the Mohegan tribe of Connecticut, goes undercover posing as a contestant on a reality TV competition.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying & Selling
programme.desc: A couple with a new baby needs a bigger home, but they don't want to get saddled with a heftier mortgage. Jonathan is determined to transform the dated interior of their current home while Drew takes charge of the ambitious house hunt.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Rob and Rich, and Simon and Lauren, take on Britain's strongest man, 28-stone Eddie `The Beast' Hall, and his wife Alex, as they compete to host the best dinner party in Stoke-on-Trent.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Stockport, Ian and Lithuanian-born Loreta go back to their roots. 'Gemily' offer a taste of Morocco. Tim and Luan serve their favourite dishes - plus some loo roll origami entertainment.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Luke, 22, and Alex, 25, met ten months ago when working on Ibiza. Despite not being together for very long, they knew they are made for each other. So after just six months, Luke decided to propose. Money is tight, but this hasn't stopped Alex dreaming of a traditional wedding, surrounded by all her friends and family. However, Luke has got very different ideas and tries to pull off the best Ibiza wedding ever.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One with the Big Kid's Wedding
programme.desc: Kara, 25, is moving out of her house in Reading and leaving the keys in the tattooed hands of her 22-year-old fiancé, Alex. Kara is petrified about leaving him home alone in her house, especially as he has a reputation of partying hard and being so bad with money that she even has to babysit his bankcard. Alex is a care worker and has never organised anything this big before.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: After a 25-year break from working as a chauffeur, EmpireCLS CEO David Seelinger goes under cover to see if his business is running smoothly.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: The Rocket Fizz Soda Pop and Candy Shops President Robert Powells makes a discovery about his inventory system while under cover.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Ramsay revisits Boston's La Galleria, the Olde Hitching Post in Hanson, Mass., and the Prohibition Gastropub in Everett, Wash.

programme.title: Ramsay's Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels in the USA, aiming to fix hopeless hotels, mediocre motels and bed & breakfasts with bad standards. In the first programme, Gordon visits Coeur d'Alene, Idaho, to try to rescue the failing Roosevelt Inn. Despite being over a million dollars in debt, the Roosevelt's owner, John Hough, is more interested in telling jokes and dressing up as Sherlock Holmes than he is in fixing his struggling B&B.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying & Selling
programme.desc: Dana and Andy ripped apart their home for an all-out overhaul, but then Andy got a new job, the huge project stalled, and now they're stuck. Jonathan steps in to finish the job, however, he soon uncovers a host of scary and expensive fixes.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In north London, married couple Elorm and Mimi are confident that their Afro-fusion menu and some Afro-beats will see them triumph. They are joined by Kiri and Dale, and Serge and Eleni.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: Greg Davies has witnessed many bizarre moments during his time as the Taskmaster, from oars for arms to a beatboxing wolf. Nick Mohammed won't stop winking.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the panel game, with Sean Lock and Henning Wehn who take on special guest captain Bill Bailey with Jessica Hynes. Susie Dent is joined by Tom Allen in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters.

programme.title: Friday Night Dinner
programme.desc: Mum's excited about the new curtains, but the boys and Grandma think they're horrible, so Mum is annoyed. Meanwhile, Dad drops Mum's beef casserole all over the kitchen floor and it looks disgusting. Dad scoops it back into the dish, but the boys are not happy about their meal. Later, the argument about the curtains spreads, and Jonny leaves in a huff to go to the pub.

programme.title: Friday Night Dinner
programme.desc: When Mum finds out that Dad didn't throw away his old boxes of science magazines in their clear-out, she goes mad. So Dad builds a bonfire and promises he'll burn them all, but Dad is only pretending to burn them, instead squirrelling the boxes off to his shed in the garden. Mum is excited as her best friend, Val is popping around with her new dress for her daughter Karen's wedding.

programme.title: Peep Show
programme.desc: Mark and Jeremy go on the pull and find themselves smoking pot in the Lazerbowl toilets with a teenage goth. Jeremy perseveres with the sexy Toni despite her refusal to stop talking about Peter Gabriel, and after a few pints Mark decides a melon is a big improvement on a bowling ball.

programme.title: Peep Show
programme.desc: Mark makes a friend at a business conference - but will it turn into more than just friendship? Meanwhile, Jeremy is forced to consider Super Hans as a replacement flatmate - and resolves to stop at nothing to keep Mark and himself together.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Extreme
programme.desc: Sketch invalidates Kieran's raunchy request. Alice spices up superfan Hugo's life. Pash covers Shelly's preposterous poem. Uzzi reins in Craig's horrible horse.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: Greg Davies has witnessed many bizarre moments during his time as the Taskmaster, from oars for arms to a beatboxing wolf. Nick Mohammed won't stop winking.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the panel game, with Sean Lock and Henning Wehn who take on special guest captain Bill Bailey with Jessica Hynes. Susie Dent is joined by Tom Allen in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Joe DiDomizio is the president and CEO of Hudson Group, a travel retailer that operates newsstands, bookshops, cafés and speciality stores in 70 airports and other transportation terminals throughout North America.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Utah Jazz CEO Greg Miller's team spirit is tested when he is asked to be a human basketball hoop while he is undercover.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying & Selling
programme.desc: A woman who has beaten cancer wants to be able to live the rest of her life to the fullest, and the boys must help her find the dream space to do just that. Jonathan turns her home into a showstopper and Drew's house hunt ends up close to square one.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Laura and James, Sarah and Dan, and Edward and Carol compete to throw the best dinner party in Surrey, in an edition featuring rapping, monobrows and surprising revelations.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Bromley, Ryan and Julie's food fails to impress, with one guest comparing the main to vomit! Can Jo and Alex or Michelle and Craig do better with their fine dining and Caribbean menus?

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Ex-footballer Steven, 26, tries so hard to pull off the wedding of his dreams, but when he's confronted with his furious bride-to-be Kaleigh on their wedding day, does he finally realise he's made a huge mistake? Steven decides to have their wedding at Thorpe Park, but his idea of putting Kaleigh on the Stealth ride before they've even tied the knot comes crashing down when she refuses to do it.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One at a Naval College
programme.desc: Essex boy Andrew, 27, is a groom torn between two worlds. On one side is his former boss in the Royal Navy and bride-to-be Amanda, who wants Andrew to man up and take charge of the wedding. Although Amanda left the navy three years ago, she still gives the orders in their relationship, but in his desperation to please her, will he make the wrong decision?

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Rick Forman, founder and CEO of New Jersey-based Forman Mills, a discount clothing superstore, goes incognito among his own staff posing as a contestant on a television reality show.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Stella & Dot CEO Jessica Herrin goes under cover to find the real gems working in her company, and learns one employee's true feelings about corporate management.

programme.title: Ramsay's Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Cambridge, New York, where ex-military man John Imhof and his wife, Tina, own and operate the allegedly haunted Cambridge Hotel.

programme.title: Ramsay's Hotel Hell
programme.desc: The first part of a two-part visit to the Juniper Hill Inn in Windsor, Vermont, a place that looks like a million dollars but smells of raw sewage. Gordon meets the inn's wealthy owner, Robert Dean II, and uncovers some shocking treatment towards the staff. Gordon quickly realises that it's going to need something approaching a miracle if he's going to have a chance of saving the place.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying & Selling
programme.desc: Recently widowed, Rosanne is selling the suburban home she bought with her late partner Joe and is looking for a cosy downtown property. In order to get the most from the sale, Jonathan steps in to transform the property.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Essex, Karen and DJ Paul, outdoorsy couple Lucy and her partner Paul, and Pat and her husband Tino compete in a week featuring a guest who is left hungry and an unconventional dessert.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: Little Alex Horne risks raising Greg Davies' hackles with a task in celebration of himself. Steve Pemberton has an expletive-laden row with a bin, and a novel use for a cow's ear.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts, as Sean Lock and Jason Manford go up against Jon Richardson and Roisin Conaty. Sam Simmons joins Countdown's resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters.

programme.title: Friday Night Dinner
programme.desc: It's Adam's birthday and as he arrives home, a silver Mercedes almost runs him over. Inside, is Sheila Bloom. She is famously obsessed with her Mercedes, and after some catty remarks about Adam's `Yugo', she speeds off. Dad reveals that he used to have a thing with Sheila years ago, the boys can't believe it and Mum's not happy. Later, the family abandon dinner and go for a Chinese, but Sheila is there.

programme.title: Friday Night Dinner
programme.desc: Adam comes home to find the house looking immaculate, with classical music playing in the living room, Mum's posh bowls on the dinner table, Dad in a smart suit, and most mysteriously - the table is set for five. The doorbell rings and in comes Tanya Green, a girl Mum has been trying to fix up with Adam for months. What follows is possibly the most excruciating family dinner scene of all time.

programme.title: Peep Show
programme.desc: Jeremy tries to wow Toni with his new job in a music studio while Mark gets passed over for promotion in favour of Sophie. They then attempt to solve each other's problems with the help of phone stalking and pepper spray.

programme.title: Peep Show
programme.desc: Death and terminal illness prove to be the keys to romantic success for Mark and Jeremy. Jeremy gets in with the Christians, while Mark finds a funeral to be the perfect venue for a first date with Sophie.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers on Holiday
programme.desc: Ian needs help from Sketch to cover his Magaluf monstrosity, Alice helps Aston with his ugly Ibiza sunset, and Jay gives Callum a portrait to remember on his final holiday before he joins the army.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: Little Alex Horne risks raising Greg Davies' hackles with a task in celebration of himself. Steve Pemberton has an expletive-laden row with a bin, and a novel use for a cow's ear.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts, as Sean Lock and Jason Manford go up against Jon Richardson and Roisin Conaty. Sam Simmons joins Countdown's resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Barry and Adam bond over the discovery of a TV channel, and Beverly involves herself in her father's dating life.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam, Erica and Barry learn about Murray's romantic past while cleaning out the garage. Meanwhile, Adam falls for his neighbour.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride: Revisited
programme.desc: Ellis and Luke remember their unusual rave-themed wedding from way back in 2013 along with best man Afro and Ellis' straight-talking mum Dee, who discuss whether the boys have been forgiven for their crazy decisions.

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Prince is smitten with Vicky, but does she feel the same? Warren makes a chilling discovery, and Nancy reaches breaking point.

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Mercedes sees a side to Jenson that she doesn't like, and Myra finds a hidden camera in the house. Elsewhere, things look up for one family, while panic sets in for another.

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube
programme.desc: A short clip from Rude Tube's naughty little sister Rude-ish Tube, featuring food and animals.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In south Wales, beautician Rachael and her partner Nick take a risk by cooking dishes they've never made before, and Ameel shows off his powers of memory recall.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon visits Italian bistro Charlie's in La Verne, Southern California, which was bought in 2010 by Tatiana Leiva, her mother and her sister. Tatiana didn't actually put any money into the purchase herself, but her hard-working mother and sister did because they felt they were helping to fulfil Tatiana's dream. They even put up their houses as collateral! Unfortunately, soon Charlie's was in trouble.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to Baltimore to try to turn around a restaurant that is suffering because its owner, Denise Whiting, has managed to alienate the surrounding community. When Denise opened Café Hon in 1992 it was a roaring success, but as a result of her unwavering business ambition the local community decided to boycott the restaurant, leaving Denise frustrated by falling sales.

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.desc: The newly-weds are brought back to earth with a bump when they move in together and start facing the challenges - and arguments - of regular married life.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Jay and Manny set out to cut down their own Christmas tree. Meanwhile, Gloria is on edge when her mother visits. And Mitchell does last-minute shopping to find the perfect gift for Lily.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Phil ends up in jail after tricking Luke into taking a dance class. Meanwhile, Jay and Gloria plan birthday parties for Manny and Joe. Later, Mitch and Cam try to book a wedding venue.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge and Homer celebrate their 11th anniversary, but find that the sexual spark is gone. Meanwhile, Bart and Lisa go treasure hunting and find an alternative ending to a classic film.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer's evil hammock produces an army of Homer clones, zombie cowboys threaten to destroy Springfield after Lisa outlaws guns, and the Simpsons travel to an island inhabited by manimals.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: How I Spent My Strummer Vacation
programme.desc: Marge and the kids send Homer off to rock `n' roll camp to realise his dream of being a rock star.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: Bart vs Lisa vs the 3rd Grade
programme.desc: Lisa has competition for the class brain role when she is promoted to the third grade. It comes from Bart, who's been demoted to the same class.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Peralta and Santiago's ongoing bet about who can make the most arrests comes to an embarrassing conclusion. Meanwhile, Boyle receives a medal after he's shot in the line of duty.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: For his first task back in the field, Sergeant Jeffords prepares to join Peralta and Boyle in infiltrating a gym where steroids are being sold illegally.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: When Claire lets Phil take charge of Halloween, he decides to do something different than her usual fright-fest theme, Jay wants to be Prince Charming for Halloween -- complete with a full head of hair and Cam becomes overwhelmed.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Haley tries to land a job with an influential stylist, while Gloria hires a hot Spanish teacher for Manny.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Claire has her doubts when Phil steps up to the plate to cook Thanksgiving dinner. Plus, roles are reversed after Cam is forced to play bad cop.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Jay and Gloria celebrate their anniversary but can't decide on what kind of gift to give. The Dunphys, meanwhile, join Alex on her college tour.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart wages war against Milhouse when they are enrolled in different activity clubs.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer and Marge permit Moe to babysit Maggie after she takes a shine to the bartender.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Once Ned and Homer's bounty hunting partnership takes a turn for the worse, Homer becomes the hunted, as Ned is on the prowl for him after he skips bail. Meanwhile, Marge mistakenly takes a job at an erotic bakery.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A believer in the mantra finders keepers, a sticky-fingered Bart steals actor Denis Leary's phone and uses it for pranking purposes.

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: The police give the Osbornes some worrying news. Two residents decide to move forward together. And when Warren steps in to help Mercedes, will sparks re-ignite?

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Springfield decides to install a monorail system, Homer gets the job as conductor, but has no idea what he's doing, although he does get to wear a cape. When the monorail opens, all hell breaks loose and it's down to Homer and Bart to save the passengers.

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.desc: After an explosive meeting with an expert, one couple are left to pick up the pieces of their relationship. But is another pair's marriage already beyond fixing?

programme.title: Celebs Go Dating
programme.desc: PK Humble gets himself in trouble with the agents before going on a date. Stephen meets Claira for a make-or-break chat. Will Lucinda feel flutters with Oli?

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britain's sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the week's biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson leads people through the prickly business of choosing a partner naked. First into the studio is 21-year-old Dom, from Hampshire, who's been nominated for the show by his mum, in a desperate attempt to break his dismal dating record.

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Business development manager Emily and part-time model Matt look like a good match, until talk turns to aliens, and 73-year-old Michael's roaming eye gets him into trouble.

programme.desc: PK Humble gets himself in trouble with the agents before going on a date. Stephen meets Claira for a make-or-break chat. Will Lucinda feel flutters with Oli?

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to Fremont, California, to try to save a restaurant that is tearing the owners' marriage apart. Saki Kavouniaris took over the failing Spin-a-Yarn restaurant in 1995 and began to turn it into a success. He fell in love with a waitress, but the restaurant and the marriage are now failing.
